keyDevelopments: New administration's first six months established policy direction across all domains
Initial executive orders on immigration, trade, and regulatory reform set the tone
DOJ leadership transition completed with new priorities announced; 17 inspectors general fired in January

changes.summary: Escalation of executive actions and early institutional friction
keyDevelopments: Courts ruled mass IG firings unlawful but declined reinstatement, establishing pattern of unenforceable rulings
Liberation Day tariffs imposed 10% baseline on all imports with higher rates for China; trade war escalation began
CIA offered buyouts to entire workforce; intelligence community independence concerns emerged

categoryChanges.rationale: CIA workforce buyout offer and early signs of intelligence politicization; DNI began reassigning analysts producing inconvenient assessments
Liberation Day tariffs announced; 10% baseline on all imports with country-specific rates up to 49% for China
Firing of 17 inspectors general in single action; courts ruled action unlawful but declined to order reinstatement; DOGE gained access to sensitive federal payment and personnel systems

changes.summary: Intelligence community concerns emerge; regulatory shifts accelerate
keyDevelopments: Sen. Warner's floor speech warning of systematic undermining of intelligence community independence
Schedule F executive order reinstated, reclassifying career civil service positions as at-will
Voting restrictions advanced in multiple states ahead of 2026 midterms

categoryChanges.rationale: Multiple states advanced proof-of-citizenship requirements; DOJ began requesting voter registration files from states
DOJ restructuring under AG Bondi accelerated; career ethics officials purged; politically-motivated case decisions increased
January 6 pardons for ~1,500 defendants including seditious conspiracy convictions generated significant public division

changes.summary: Election administration concerns intensify; university funding threats emerge
keyDevelopments: 16 states enacted 29 new restrictive voting laws, with 28 taking effect before 2026 midterms
University funding threats escalated with Harvard grant terminations totaling $2.2B
DOGE-led contract terminations exceeded 6,000; government shutdown briefly halted operations

categoryChanges.rationale: Government operating under continuing resolutions; DOGE workforce reductions beginning to impact agency capacity
FCC opened investigations into ABC, NBC, CBS over election coverage; Paramount settled with Trump under merger pressure; Harvard funding terminated

changes.summary: Judicial conflicts escalate; regulatory and contracting concerns emerge
keyDevelopments: Deputy AG declared administration 'at war' with federal judiciary at Federalist Society conference
100+ DOJ prosecutors resigned citing political interference; threats against judges tripled
Trade tariffs on China reached 145%; retaliatory tariffs at 125%; trade deficit widened $175B

categoryChanges.rationale: Executive order count exceeded 200; AI EO reversed Biden-era safeguards; contradictory guidance created compliance chaos
DOGE-led contract consolidation exceeded 6,000 terminations; White House ballroom donors with $279B in contracts identified

changes.summary: Federal Reserve independence threatened; rule of law and national security escalate
keyDevelopments: Unprecedented attempt to fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook; DOJ probe of Federal Reserve announced
Intelligence Authorization Act included politicization safeguards, reflecting bipartisan concern
Administration appointed Stephen Miran to Fed board while retaining admin position; sole dissent on rates

categoryChanges.rationale: Administration pattern of delayed compliance with court orders crystallized across immigration, environmental, and civil rights cases
DNI fired National Intelligence Council officials over Venezuela assessment; intelligence resources diverted to Greenland proposal

changes.summary: One-year mark shows sustained institutional pressure across all domains
keyDevelopments: All categories show 'increasing' trend direction at the one-year mark
Rule of Law domain highest; 235 executive orders signed in first year
Cumulative effect of 108 restrictive voting laws since 2020 becoming clear ahead of midterms

categoryChanges.rationale: Cumulative effect of 108 restrictive laws since 2020; 28 new restrictions taking effect before 2026 midterms; Trump called for 'nationalizing' voting

changes.summary: Largest monthly increase: Minneapolis crisis, SCOTUS tariffs ruling, Pentagon purge, Fed criminal probe, and CPB dissolution drive broad escalation
keyDevelopments: Two U.S. citizens killed by ICE/CBP in Minneapolis; nationwide general strike and protests in 300+ cities; DHS admitted agents lied under oath

SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs 6-3 but also eliminated nationwide injunctions; CIA retracted 19 intelligence reports at direction of political appointees; Pentagon purged Joint Chiefs chairman and multiple service chiefs
DOJ criminal investigation of Fed Chair Powell (first in 113 years); CPB dissolved ending NPR/PBS funding; Schedule F finalized; FBI raided Washington Post reporter's home

categoryChanges.rationale: Schedule F finalized (effective March 8) reclassifying 50,000 employees as at-will; EPA revoked greenhouse gas endangerment finding; SCOTUS allowing firing of independent agency heads; EO total reaches 243
DOJ criminal investigation of Fed Chair Powell — first in 113 years; two government shutdowns; DOGE cuts estimated to cost $135B; IRS projects $8.5B revenue loss from 22,000 departures
Don Lemon arrested on federal charges for covering protest; FBI raided WaPo reporter's home seizing all devices; CPB dissolved ending $1.1B in NPR/PBS funding; FCC weaponizing equal time rules
Two U.S. citizens killed by federal agents; DHS admitted agents lied under oath; Minnesota general strike; 457 judges targeted with threats (150% increase); 33+ pardoned Jan 6 defendants rearrested
322,000+ federal employees departed (largest reduction in US history); 75%+ IG positions vacant; 8,000+ web pages and 3,000 datasets removed from public access; layoff moratorium expired

changes.summary: ICE court order violations quantified; tariff authority crisis deepens; DHS shutdown extends
keyDevelopments: Court of International Trade ordered nationwide refund of $166B in IEEPA tariffs; Section 122 replacement tariffs at highest effective rate since 1943; J.P. Morgan puts recession probability at 40-50%
Chief Judge Schiltz documented 210 ICE violations across 143 cases; 100+ DOJ prosecutors resigned; retributive cases against Comey and James dismissed as illegally brought
DHS partial shutdown in 5th week affecting 260,000 personnel; deficit hit $1T in first 5 months of FY2026; Fed confirmation process blocked by bipartisan coalition

categoryChanges.rationale: 210 documented ICE court order violations in one jurisdiction quantifies systematic defiance; mass DOJ prosecutor exodus; retributive prosecutions dismissed as illegally brought
Constitutional crisis over tariff authority: IEEPA struck down, emergency pivot to untested Section 122, $166B refund order, 24-state lawsuit; 40-50% recession probability

changes.summary: Risk crosses into High territory: Trump signs mail-ballot EO, DHS shutdown becomes longest on record, Iran oil shock amplifies recession risk, DOGE contract terminations reach $120B, university funding freezes accelerate
keyDevelopments: Trump signed EO directing USPS to gatekeep mail-in ballots (March 31); DOJ voter roll lawsuits expanded to 30+ states; Florida, South Dakota, Utah enacted proof-of-citizenship laws
DHS shutdown reached day 47 (longest on record); Iran conflict pushed oil past $120/barrel; Moody's recession probability at 49%; 'No Kings' March 28 protest drew 8-9 million (largest in US history)

DOGE terminated $120B in contracts/grants with zero cuts to Musk-linked companies; university funding freezes expanded to Brown, Cornell, Northwestern, Princeton ($2.5B+ new); DEI contractor certification EO requires anti-DEI compliance within 30 days

categoryChanges.rationale: March 31 EO directing USPS to gatekeep mail ballots; DOJ expanded voter roll demands to 30+ states; three states enacted proof-of-citizenship laws
DOGE contract terminations reached $120B with zero cuts to Musk-linked companies; FAA/Starlink procurement directed verbally to avoid paper trail
